title: chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. glandulosum
intro: Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. glandulosum, also known as the golden buttons, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to Australia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. glandulosum include reducing inflammation and pain, treating wounds and infections, and providing relief from digestive issues and stress. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from the leaves, flowers, and stems, which can be used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
